headline: Day 7: 1,230+ Dead in Iran; House Backs Trump; Navy 'Gone'
tldr: Day 7 of Operation Epic Fury. At least 1,230 killed in Iran per AP. Trump says Iran's navy, air force, air defense, and communications are 'gone.' 24 ships sunk in three days. House voted to let the war continue, rejecting a bipartisan resolution to curb Trump's authority. Hegseth says munitions are 'full up.'
alliances_short: Joint operations with Israel. UK offering bases. NATO activated ballistic missile defense. House rejected war powers challenge.
forecast: With Congress backing the war and stockpiles reportedly full, expect sustained and intensifying strikes. Iran's conventional military being systematically dismantled. Ground troop question remains open.
current_events: Day 7 of Operation Epic Fury. At least 1,230 people killed in Iran according to the Associated Press. Trump told reporters the US is 'totally demolishing' Iranian forces 'far ahead of schedule.' He detailed Iran's losses: 24 ships sunk in three days, no air force, no air defense, all airplanes gone, communications gone, 60% of missiles and 64% of launchers destroyed. Defense Secretary Hegseth visited CENTCOM in Tampa and declared: 'We have no shortage of munitions. Our stockpiles are full up and our will is ironclad.' Admiral Cooper confirmed Iran has now attacked 12 countries. The House voted to allow Operation Epic Fury to continue, defeating the Massie-Khanna resolution that sought to curb Trump's war authority. Trump called on IRGC, military, and police to 'lay down their arms' — saying 'they're only going to be killed.' NATO activated heightened ballistic missile defense posture across the alliance. A girls' school attack in southern Iran killed 165-168 people, drawing international condemnation.
alliances: Full coordination with Israel. UK bases available for defensive strikes. NATO activated heightened ballistic missile defense. GCC coalition condemning Iran. House of Representatives backed Trump's authority to continue operations. 12 countries now attacked by Iran.
next_actions: With Congressional backing secured and munitions described as full, the campaign will intensify. Watch for whether Trump's call for IRGC surrender gains any traction. Ground troop deployment remains the key escalation question.

headline: Preparing Ground Invasion South of Litani River; 800,000 Ordered to Evacuate
tldr: Israel preparing a full ground invasion of southern Lebanon south of the Litani River. IDF issued evacuation orders affecting 800,000+ people, including 500,000 in southern Beirut. IDF Chief of Staff says 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed. Ground clashes already underway near Khiyam.
alliances_short: Joint Iran strikes destroying 80% of air defenses. Lebanon ground invasion being prepared. Three IDF divisions deployed along border.
forecast: Full-scale ground invasion of southern Lebanon imminent — likely within days. Massive humanitarian crisis as 800,000+ ordered to evacuate. Two-front war at maximum intensity.
current_events: Israel is preparing a full ground invasion of southern Lebanon south of the Litani River. On March 4, IDF spokesman Avichay Adraee issued broad evacuation orders telling all civilians to move north of the Litani — affecting an estimated 250-300 settlements including the cities of Tyre and Nabatieh. On March 5, evacuation orders expanded to 500,000 people in southern Beirut ahead of planned bombing of Hezbollah strongholds. Total evacuation orders now affect 800,000+ people. Defense Minister Katz instructed the IDF, with Netanyahu's approval, to occupy additional positions inside Lebanon. Three IDF divisions — the 91st, 210th, and 146th — are deployed along the border. Ground clashes already occurred near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra on March 4, with Hezbollah claiming to have repelled an Israeli incursion. IDF Chief of Staff reported destroying 'about 80% of air defense systems' in Iran and achieving 'almost complete air superiority in Iranian skies.' More than 60% of ballistic missile launchers neutralized. At least 250 Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon since Hezbollah entered the war. Targeted assassinations of Hezbollah, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and Jamaa Islamiya commanders continue.
alliances: Full US coordination on Iran. Three IDF divisions positioned for Lebanon ground invasion. Lebanese government still demanding Hezbollah disarm. International community watching closely.
next_actions: Ground invasion of southern Lebanon imminent — the 800,000-person evacuation order is the clearest signal. Watch for IDF movement along three corridors: Western (Ras al-Naqoura), Eastern (Khiyam), and Middle (Aita ech Chaab). Humanitarian catastrophe looming.

headline: 1,230+ Dead; Navy Destroyed; Girls' School Attack Kills 168; Hormuz Closed
tldr: Death toll exceeds 1,230 per AP. Iran's navy effectively destroyed (24 ships sunk). Air force and air defense 'gone.' 168 killed in attack on girls' school. Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, threatening to set ablaze any vessel. Leadership council functioning but under immense pressure. Attacked 12 countries.
alliances_short: Russia offers only rhetoric. Proxy network active — Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi militias all engaged. But Iran's conventional military is being systematically dismantled.
forecast: Iran's conventional military capability is collapsing. The proxy network is now the primary instrument of retaliation. Hormuz closure creates unprecedented global energy crisis. Succession battle continues under fire.
current_events: Iran's death toll has exceeded 1,230 according to the Associated Press. The Iranian military has suffered catastrophic losses: 24 naval ships sunk in three days, air force destroyed, air defense systems 80% degraded, communications destroyed, 60% of missiles and 64% of launchers eliminated. A devastating attack on a girls' school in southern Iran killed 165-168 people, drawing international condemnation and Red Cross concern about civilian protection. The ICRC released audio of a Tehran civilian describing 'constant explosions and no respite.' Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, warning any vessels attempting passage would be 'set ablaze.' The three-man leadership council (Pezeshkian, Arafi, Mohseni-Ejei) is functioning but under immense pressure. Iran has now attacked 12 countries in retaliation. Trump called on the IRGC to 'lay down their arms.'
alliances: Russia continues offering only rhetoric. China coordinating with Russia on UNSC response. Iran's proxy network — Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi militias — is the primary remaining offensive capability as conventional forces are destroyed.
next_actions: Iran's conventional military capability is collapsing — the proxy network becomes its primary weapon. Hormuz closure is an unprecedented escalation affecting 20% of global oil. Watch for the succession battle and whether IRGC hardliners or the leadership council control Iran's next moves.

headline: Second Drone Strike on Ras Tanura Thwarted; GCC Invokes UN Self-Defense
tldr: A second drone strike on Ras Tanura refinery was intercepted without damage. GCC invoked Article 51 of the UN Charter (collective self-defense), established 'red lines,' and activated joint air-defense systems. Gulf states now conducting reconnaissance flights across regional airspace.
alliances_short: GCC invoking UN Charter self-defense. Joint air defense activated. Gulf solidarity strengthened by Iranian attacks. US evacuating non-essential staff.
forecast: GCC moving from defensive posture toward potential 'active theater of response.' Continued Iranian strikes could push Gulf states into the anti-Iran coalition as active participants.
current_events: A second drone attack on the Ras Tanura oil refinery was successfully intercepted without damage or operational disruption. The GCC held emergency meetings and invoked Article 51 of the UN Charter (collective self-defense), established 'red lines,' and signaled readiness for collective defense. Gulf states activated joint air-defense systems and initiated reconnaissance flights across regional airspace. Analysts warn that continued Iranian strikes could transform the Gulf from a 'defensive barrier' into an 'active theater of response.' The UAE has been particularly affected — 63% of Iranian strikes targeted its airports, ports, and oil infrastructure. Within 48 hours, around 165 ballistic/cruise missiles and 600 drones were launched at the UAE alone. The UAE recalled its ambassador from Tehran and closed its embassy. Iranian strikes hit Dubai luxury hotels, Amazon data centers, and airports — damaging the Gulf states' image as stable business hubs.
alliances: GCC solidarity strengthened — joint condemnation, Article 51 invocation, joint air defense activation. US defense partnership under strain. Saudi Arabia hosting US troops under fire. UAE recalled ambassador from Iran.
next_actions: Watch for whether GCC states transition from defense to active participation in the anti-Iran coalition. Energy infrastructure remains the top vulnerability. Gulf neutrality is effectively over.

headline: 800,000+ Ordered to Evacuate; Israel Ground Invasion Imminent
tldr: Israel issued evacuation orders affecting 800,000+ people — civilians told to move north of the Litani River. Three IDF divisions positioned for ground invasion. 250+ Israeli airstrikes since Hezbollah entered the war. Ground clashes already occurring near Khiyam. Humanitarian catastrophe unfolding.
alliances_short: PM Salam opposing Hezbollah. Israel preparing ground invasion with three divisions. Massive displacement underway.
forecast: Full-scale Israeli ground invasion of southern Lebanon imminent within days. Humanitarian catastrophe as 800,000+ displaced. Hezbollah-IDF ground clashes intensifying.
current_events: Israel issued sweeping evacuation orders affecting 800,000+ people in southern Lebanon and southern Beirut. Civilians were told to move north of the Litani River — the order covers 250-300 settlements including the major cities of Tyre and Nabatieh. On March 5, 500,000 in southern Beirut were ordered to evacuate ahead of planned strikes on Hezbollah strongholds. Three IDF divisions (91st, 210th, 146th) are deployed for what analysts describe as an imminent ground invasion. Ground clashes already occurred near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra on March 4, with Hezbollah militants engaging Israeli forces. Israel has conducted at least 250 airstrikes in Lebanon since Hezbollah entered the war. Targeted assassinations of Hezbollah, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and Jamaa Islamiya commanders continue. CNN reports intensifying crossfire on the Israel-Lebanon border as of March 6.
alliances: Lebanese PM Salam continues opposing Hezbollah. International community watching as massive displacement unfolds. The evacuation orders are the most significant since the 2006 war.
next_actions: Ground invasion likely along three corridors: Western Sector (Ras al-Naqoura), Eastern Sector (Khiyam), Middle Sector (Aita ech Chaab). Humanitarian crisis will worsen dramatically. Hezbollah fortified villages will be contested.

headline: Dual Chokepoint Crisis: Hormuz Closed + Houthis Resume Red Sea Attacks
tldr: An unprecedented dual chokepoint crisis: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz while Houthis announced resumed Red Sea attacks. Both Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb threatened simultaneously. Maersk already rerouting ships via Cape of Good Hope. UKMTO issued security advisories. No confirmed new maritime strike yet.
alliances_short: Iran's proxy network fully activated. Houthis retained full capabilities during ceasefire pause. Anti-Houthi coalition distracted by Iran war.
forecast: The dual chokepoint crisis (Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb) is unprecedented and will cause the most severe global shipping disruption in decades. Maritime insurance premiums already spiking.
current_events: An unprecedented dual chokepoint crisis has emerged. Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz (20% of global oil transits), warning vessels would be 'set ablaze.' Simultaneously, the Houthis announced resumed Red Sea shipping attacks, ending a 3.5-month pause since mid-November 2025. Senior Houthi officials confirmed renewed attacks could begin 'immediately.' This creates a simultaneous threat to both the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el-Mandeb Strait — the two most critical maritime chokepoints for global energy and trade. Maersk confirmed ships will reroute via Cape of Good Hope. BIMCO issued industry warnings about targeting patterns and insurance escalation. UKMTO published formal security advisories. The UN Security Council adopted Resolution 2812 extending Houthi attack reporting requirements. Military analysts note the Houthis retained full drone and missile capabilities during the pause and can resume at previous intensity with minimal preparation.
alliances: Part of Iran's 'Axis of Resistance.' Anti-Houthi coalition (Saudi, UAE, US) is now focused on the broader Iran war. The Houthis are exploiting this distraction to resume maritime operations.
next_actions: First confirmed new maritime strike will be the trigger for full Red Sea crisis. Watch for Maersk and CMA CGM formal rerouting announcements. Global shipping costs, insurance premiums, and delivery times will spike dramatically.

headline: Using Iran Crisis to Bash US; Ukraine Hits Russian Naval Base and Oil
tldr: Russian officials using the Iran war to harshly criticize the US and rhetorically position Russia and Iran together. Ukraine damaged a Russian frigate and warships at a naval base. Ukraine struck oil infrastructure in Saratov Oblast (750+ miles from the border). Hungary-Ukraine tensions flare over Druzhba pipeline.
alliances_short: Using Iran crisis for anti-US propaganda but still offering no material support. Ukraine striking deep into Russia. Hungary-Ukraine tensions rising.
forecast: Russia will continue leveraging the Iran crisis diplomatically while unable to help materially. Ukraine's deep strikes and naval attacks demonstrate growing reach. Hungary-Ukraine friction is a new wildcard.
current_events: Russian officials are using US military operations against Iran to harshly criticize the United States and rhetorically place Russia alongside Iran as victims of Western aggression. However, material support remains zero. On the battlefield: Ukrainian forces damaged a Russian frigate and other warships during overnight strikes on March 1-2 against a Russian naval base. Ukraine also struck oil infrastructure near the Saratov Oil Refinery — over 750 miles from the border — demonstrating continued deep-strike capability. Russian forces continued offensive operations across multiple axes (Sumy, Kharkiv, Pokrovsk, Dobropillya, Hulyaipole) but made no confirmed advances on March 5. Russian forces launched 155 Shaheds and other drones overnight March 4-5, of which Ukraine downed 136. A new diplomatic dispute emerged between Hungary and Ukraine over the Druzhba pipeline, with Ukraine's FM saying Hungary detained seven Ukrainian bank employees.
alliances: Russia-Iran 'alliance' remains rhetorical only. China coordination on UNSC continues. North Korea providing troops and ammunition. European nations pursuing shadow fleet. Hungary-Ukraine tensions add a new intra-European friction point.
next_actions: Watch for Russia exploiting the Iran crisis in peace talks. Ukraine's deep strikes and naval attacks may escalate. Hungary-Ukraine Druzhba pipeline dispute could complicate European unity.

headline: Damaged Russian Warships; Deep Strikes on Oil Infrastructure; 136 Drones Downed
tldr: Ukraine damaged a Russian frigate at a naval base. Struck Saratov oil infrastructure 750+ miles from border. Downed 136 of 155 Russian drones in one night. Russia unable to advance on any axis March 5. Hungary-Ukraine diplomatic spat over Druzhba pipeline bank employees.
alliances_short: Western coalition strong. Drone warfare expertise shared with Gulf. Deep-strike capability growing. Hungary friction is new complication.
forecast: Ukraine continues demonstrating increasing reach and capability. The Iran crisis provides both leverage (drone expertise) and risk (reduced Western attention). Hungary dispute adds European tension.
current_events: Ukraine damaged a Russian frigate and other warships during overnight strikes March 1-2 against a Russian naval base — demonstrating growing naval strike capability. Ukraine struck oil infrastructure near the Saratov Oil Refinery, over 750 miles from the border, with footage showing explosions and fires. Ukraine's air force downed 136 of 155 Russian drones (mostly Shaheds) launched overnight March 4-5. Russian forces continued offensive operations across Sumy, Kharkiv, Pokrovsk, Dobropillya, and Hulyaipole directions on March 5 but made no confirmed advances on any axis. ISW assessed Russian forces have 'decreased intensity of infiltration efforts' near Pokrovsk, possibly due to insufficient unmanned ground vehicles for logistics. A diplomatic dispute with Hungary erupted over the Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil — Hungary detained seven Ukrainian state bank employees.
alliances: Western coalition supporting continued operations. UK facilitating drone expertise transfer to Gulf states. Trilateral talks may move to Florida. Hungary-Ukraine tensions add intra-European friction.
next_actions: Watch for continued deep strikes and naval attacks. The Hungary-Druzhba dispute could affect European energy politics. Russia's inability to advance suggests operational exhaustion on multiple axes.

headline: NPC Sets GDP 4.5-5%; Defense Up 7%; 15th Five-Year Plan Launched
tldr: NPC opened March 5. GDP target set at 4.5-5% range (lowest in years). Defense spending up 7% to 1.91 trillion yuan ($277B). 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) prioritizes AI, chips, biotech, and energy security. Fiscal deficit at 4% of GDP — most expansionary in years.
alliances_short: Coordinating with Russia on Iran UNSC response. Xi-Trump summit March 31. Defense buildup continues. Energy security elevated by Iran war.
forecast: The lower GDP target signals Beijing acknowledging headwinds from US-China trade war and global instability. Defense spending increase funds Taiwan drills, cyber warfare, and equipment. Xi-Trump summit will define the near-term.
current_events: The NPC opened March 5 with Premier Li Qiang delivering the 2026 Government Work Report. Key announcements: GDP growth target of 4.5-5% — the lowest in years and the first range target since 2019, signaling acknowledged uncertainty from US-China trade conflict and global instability. Defense spending up 7% to 1.91 trillion yuan ($277 billion), slightly below last year's 7.2% but continuing a decade of steady buildup. The 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) prioritizes AI, advanced manufacturing, quantum technology, biomanufacturing, hydrogen/nuclear fusion, and brain-computer interfaces. R&D investment targeted to grow 7%+ annually. Fiscal policy is the most expansionary in years: deficit at 4% of GDP, 5.89 trillion yuan. Ultra-long special treasury bonds (1.3T yuan) and local government special bonds (4.4T yuan) approved. Energy security elevated by Iran war — strategic petroleum reserves provide ~100 days of cover.
alliances: Coordinating with Russia on UNSC Iran response. Xi-Trump summit set March 31-April 2. Maritime militia and PLA drills continue. South Korea tensions over trade.
next_actions: Watch for Five-Year Plan implementation details. Defense budget funds Taiwan contingency preparation. Xi-Trump summit March 31 is the defining moment. Energy security now a top-tier policy driver.

headline: China Sets Defense Budget at $277B; Arms Package Still Delayed
tldr: China's defense budget now $277 billion — 7% increase continues decade-long buildup. US arms package to Taiwan still delayed for Xi-Trump summit. Japan's 2027 invasion warning stands. Lower Chinese GDP target may reduce cross-strait economic leverage.
alliances_short: US arms sale delayed. Japan alarmed. TSMC chip dependency global leverage. China's economic slowdown adds uncertainty.
forecast: Xi-Trump summit March 31 remains the critical moment for Taiwan. China's $277B defense budget signals sustained military pressure. Lower GDP target suggests internal focus may temporarily reduce external adventurism.
current_events: China's NPC confirmed a 7% defense spending increase to 1.91 trillion yuan ($277 billion), continuing a decade of military buildup. The US arms package to Taiwan remains delayed ahead of the Xi-Trump summit (March 31-April 2). Japan's security assessment of a rising 2027 invasion risk continues to shape regional planning. China's lower GDP target (4.5-5%, down from ~5%) signals acknowledged economic headwinds — some analysts suggest internal economic focus may temporarily reduce appetite for external military adventurism, while others argue economic pressure makes nationalistic diversions more likely. PLA exercises and maritime militia drills continue near Taiwan. TSMC's chip dominance (90% of global advanced semiconductors) remains Taiwan's ultimate strategic asset.
alliances: Japan closing Pacific defense gaps. US-Taiwan relationship under strain from Xi-Trump negotiations. TSMC remains de facto security guarantee.
next_actions: Xi-Trump summit March 31 will determine near-term Taiwan policy. China's defense budget and Five-Year Plan signal long-term military trajectory. Watch for whether economic headwinds moderate or amplify cross-strait tensions.

headline: Kim Watches Cruise Missiles Launch from New Destroyer Choe Hyon
tldr: Kim Jong Un watched cruise missiles launch from North Korea's newest destroyer, the Choe Hyon, believed to carry dozens of missiles including potentially nuclear-capable weapons. This follows two earlier ballistic missile tests in 2026. Global attention on Iran provides cover for weapons development.
alliances_short: Deepening Russia ties. China economic lifeline. New destroyer demonstrates growing naval capabilities. Using Iran crisis as cover.
forecast: North Korea's naval capabilities are expanding with the Choe Hyon destroyer. The Iran crisis provides ideal cover for further weapons tests and development. Kim is methodically advancing his nuclear and missile programs while the world looks elsewhere.
current_events: Kim Jong Un watched cruise missiles launch from North Korea's newest destroyer, the Choe Hyon. The ship is believed to carry dozens of missiles, including weapons North Korea claims could be nuclear-capable. This follows two earlier ballistic missile tests in 2026 — hypersonic missiles in January that flew ~1,000 km, and another test later in January. Kim cited 'recent geopolitical crisis' as justification for 'strong and reliable nuclear deterrent.' The Workers' Party Congress five-year nuclear plan is now in effect, prioritizing increasing the number of nuclear warheads and developing AI-enabled submarine-launched missiles. North Korean troops continue fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine. The Iran crisis provides cover for advancing weapons programs while global attention is diverted.
alliances: Deepening military cooperation with Russia — troops and ammunition for technology. China remains primary economic lifeline. Kim's September summit with Xi continues to shape the relationship.
next_actions: The Choe Hyon destroyer represents a significant naval capability step. Watch for further weapons tests while Iran crisis dominates headlines. AI-enabled submarine-launched missiles are a stated development goal.

headline: UN Fact-Finding Mission: RSF Atrocities Show 'Indicators of Genocidal Path'
tldr: The Independent International Fact-Finding Mission described RSF atrocities in El Fasher as 'indicators of a genocidal path.' The UN marked 1,000 days of war. Up to 400,000 estimated killed. 12M+ displaced. El Obeid besieged from three sides. Basic services near total collapse. Security Council warned: 'Unless you act now, you will have more blood on your hands.'
alliances_short: Both sides receive foreign arms. UAE backing RSF, Egypt/Turkey backing SAF. Chad lost 7 soldiers to RSF. Global attention consumed by Iran.
forecast: The 'genocidal path' designation is the gravest finding yet. With global attention on Iran, Sudan's catastrophe will deepen. Chad border clashes signal regionalization risk.
current_events: The Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for Sudan issued a report describing RSF atrocities in and around El Fasher in late October 2025 as 'indicators of a genocidal path' — the most severe characterization to date. The UN Security Council marked 1,000 days of war with warnings of genocide, mass rape, and regional spillover. Under-Secretary-General DiCarlo described 'staggering violence and unimaginable suffering.' The former US envoy estimates up to 400,000 killed. 12M+ displaced. El Obeid besieged from three sides by the RSF. Sudan has been 'stripped of its essential lifelines' — basic services near total collapse, food largely unavailable, most health facilities exhausted. Chad reported 7 soldiers killed in confrontation with RSF elements — the conflict is spilling across borders. For 12 million women and girls, this is 'a crisis within a crisis' with 'horrific' levels of sexual violence. A civil society briefer told the Security Council: 'Unless you act now, you will have more blood on your hands.'
alliances: UAE backing RSF (rejected allegations). Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey backing SAF. Quad initiative (Egypt, Saudi, UAE, US) pursuing humanitarian truce. Chad border clashes signal regionalization. Global attention consumed by Iran.
next_actions: The 'genocidal path' finding may trigger new international action. Watch for the Quad humanitarian truce initiative. Chad border violence signals dangerous regionalization. Famine conditions spreading with no ceasefire prospect.

headline: M23 Consolidating Gains as Global Attention Focuses on Iran
tldr: M23 rebels maintain territorial control in eastern DRC. Washington Accords producing limited results. Global attention diverted to Iran. Minerals-for-security deal with US remains DRC's best leverage. 7M+ displaced.
alliances_short: Rwanda accused of backing M23. Uganda operating in east. US minerals-for-security deal under negotiation.
forecast: M23 will consolidate gains while attention is on Iran. Humanitarian crisis deepening. The minerals deal is DRC's strongest card.
current_events: Eastern DRC continues to face severe instability from armed groups, particularly the Rwanda-backed M23 rebels. M23 maintains control over significant territory in North Kivu and South Kivu. The humanitarian crisis affects over 7 million displaced civilians. The Washington Accords signed in December 2025 have produced limited results. International attention remains overwhelmingly focused on the Middle East. DRC's critical mineral wealth (70% of global cobalt, significant coltan) gives it leverage in US negotiations.
alliances: DRC accuses Rwanda of backing M23. Uganda operations in east. MONUSCO drawdown continues. US minerals-for-security deal negotiations ongoing.
next_actions: M23 consolidation likely. Watch for minerals deal progress. Humanitarian crisis worsening with global attention elsewhere.

headline: 3,200+ Released Under Amnesty; Courts 'More Organized'
tldr: Over 4,200 amnesty applications submitted. 3,200+ fully released (179 from prison, 3,052 from precautionary measures). Courts processing faster. But Foro Penal independently verified only 91-109 political releases since Feb 20. Nearly 600 remain imprisoned. Oil reserves strategically vital amid Iran crisis.
alliances_short: Operating under US influence. Amnesty process continuing. Oil reserves increasingly strategic as Iran war disrupts global supply.
forecast: Amnesty process continues but the gap between official (3,200+) and independent (91-109) numbers remains a concern. Venezuela's oil reserves are increasingly strategic leverage as global supply tightens.
current_events: Venezuela's amnesty implementation continues. Official figures show 4,203 applications submitted, with 3,232 people fully released — 179 from prison and 3,052 from precautionary measures. Courts described as 'more organized.' However, independent verification by Foro Penal confirms only 91-109 'political releases' since the law took effect — a dramatic gap between official and independent tallies. The organization seeks review of 232 excluded cases. Nearly 600 people remain in custody for political reasons. Acting President Rodríguez called the law a 'strategic tool' for 'peace, sovereignty, and mutual recognition.' Venezuela's oil reserves — the world's largest — are increasingly strategically vital as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil via the Hormuz closure.
alliances: Operating under US influence. Rodríguez cooperating on amnesty and oil reforms. Oil production is maximum leverage point as global energy crisis deepens from Iran war.
next_actions: Watch the official vs. independent verification gap. Oil industry leverage grows daily as Hormuz remains closed. Iran crisis may temporarily reduce US pressure on political reforms.

headline: Electoral Inscriptions Open March 2-12; Elections Planned August
tldr: Electoral Council opened party inscriptions March 2-12. Elections planned late August with December runoff. Police chief says security plan in development. 900 new cadets graduated. But gangs control 90% of Port-au-Prince, 1.4M displaced, 5,900+ killed in 2025.
alliances_short: US, UN, Kenya-led force providing support. Gang Suppression Force expanding. Police graduated 900 cadets but needs far more.
forecast: Elections remain aspirational. Electoral inscriptions are a positive signal but security conditions are nowhere near adequate for free elections. The gap between political timeline and security reality remains vast.
current_events: Haiti's Provisional Electoral Council opened party inscriptions March 2-12, planning general elections in late August with a December runoff. Police chief Paraison told the AP that authorities are working on a security plan but couldn't provide details. He recently oversaw graduation of 900 cadets but says more are needed — Haiti has fewer than 2 officers per 1,000 people (UN recommends 2.3). The reality remains dire: armed gangs control an estimated 90% of Port-au-Prince and are expanding into central Haiti. 1.4 million displaced. Over 5,900 killed in 2025, 2,700+ injured. More than half of Haiti's 12 million people need humanitarian support. The Kenya-led mission continues transitioning to the Gang Suppression Force. Schools, businesses, and healthcare facilities remain shuttered in gang-controlled areas.
alliances: US, UN, Japan, EU, Canada, CARICOM providing support. Kenya-led mission expanding into Gang Suppression Force. 'Haiti fatigue' among donors growing.
next_actions: Electoral inscription period closes March 12. Security planning for elections is the critical variable. Gang control of the capital makes free elections extremely difficult.
